privacy.p1: Your privacy is our top priority. All face analysis is processed locally within your browser.
privacy.p2: Images used for analysis are not sent to or stored on any server, and are deleted immediately when you refresh the page.
privacy.note: AI analysis results are for reference only and cannot replace medical diagnosis.
privacy.onDevice: On-Device Processing: Photos never leave your device.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.heroDesc: Face Symmetry is designed with a strict privacy-first approach. We believe your biometric data is yours alone. Our app is built to function entirely on your device without ever needing to upload your photos to a server.
privacyPage.noUploadsDesc: Your photos are processed locally in your browser/app memory. They are never transmitted to our servers or any third-party cloud.
privacyPage.noStorageDesc: We do not store your photos. Once you close the app or reset the analysis, the processed image data is cleared from memory.
privacyPage.noAccountsDesc: You don't need to create an account to use Face Symmetry. We don't collect your name, email, or any personal identifiers.
privacyPage.onDeviceAiDesc: The facial landmark detection and symmetry analysis are performed using on-device machine learning models (MediaPipe).
privacyPage.section1Desc: Face Symmetry **does not collect, store, or share** any personal information or biometric data. The app requires access to your camera or photo library solely to perform the analysis you request. This access is handled by the system's standard photo picker or camera interface.
privacyPage.section2Desc: Any photo you provide is used **only** for the duration of the analysis session. The mathematical landmarks extracted from your face are used to calculate symmetry scores and are discarded immediately after.
privacyPage.section3Desc: We use Google's MediaPipe for on-device facial recognition. This library runs locally and does not send your facial data to Google servers during the analysis process in this application.
privacyPage.section4Desc: Face Symmetry is a **reference analysis tool** for aesthetic and educational purposes. It is **not a medical diagnostic app**. The results provided are based on geometric calculations and should not be used for medical or surgical decisions.
privacyPage.section5Desc: If you have any questions about our privacy practices, please contact us at chars9498@gmail.com.

howItWorks.title: How AI Face Symmetry Analysis Works
howItWorks.subtitle: The Technology Behind the Balance
howItWorks.whatIsDesc: Facial symmetry is the measure of how similar the two halves of your face are to each other. While no human face is perfectly symmetrical, researchers have long studied the relationship between facial balance and perceived attractiveness, health, and genetic diversity. Our tool uses advanced computer vision to quantify these subtle differences, providing you with a detailed map of your facial structure.
howItWorks.step1Desc: The process begins with our AI engine, which uses a deep learning model called MediaPipe Face Mesh. This model detects over 468 3D facial landmarks in real-time. These landmarks are specific points on your face, such as the corners of your eyes, the bridge of your nose, and the contours of your jawline.
howItWorks.step1Note: Our AI maps these points with sub-pixel accuracy, creating a high-resolution mesh that represents your unique facial geometry. This mesh is used as the foundation for all subsequent calculations.
howItWorks.step2Desc: Once the landmarks are detected, our algorithm calculates a "Midline" for your face. This is the vertical axis that theoretically divides your face into two equal halves. We then measure the distance and angle of corresponding landmarks (e.g., left eye corner vs. right eye corner) relative to this midline.
howItWorks.step2Note: The "Overall Balance Score" is a weighted average of symmetry across four key zones: Eyes, Brows, Mouth, and Jawline. A score of 100 represents perfect mathematical symmetry, while lower scores indicate varying degrees of natural asymmetry.
howItWorks.step3Desc: It's important to understand that facial symmetry analysis is highly sensitive to external factors. To get the most accurate result, you should consider the following:
howItWorks.lightingDesc: Uneven shadows can make one side of the face appear different to the AI.
howItWorks.angleDesc: Even a slight tilt of the head can significantly impact the symmetry score.
howItWorks.expressionDesc: A smile or a wink will naturally create asymmetry in the landmarks.
howItWorks.distanceDesc: Being too close or too far from the camera can affect the perspective.
howItWorks.step4PrivacyDesc: The most critical part of our technology is where it runs. All the AI models and mathematical calculations are executed directly in your browser using your device's hardware. Your camera feed and photos are never uploaded to a server. This ensures that your biometric data remains private and secure at all times.
howItWorks.footerNote: Face Symmetry is an educational and entertainment tool designed to help you understand your facial structure. The results provided are based on mathematical analysis of 2D images and should not be used for medical or diagnostic purposes.

guide.title: Face Symmetry Guide
guide.subtitle: Understanding Facial Balance and Symmetry
guide.whatIsDesc: Facial symmetry is the measure of how similar the two halves of your face are to each other. While no human face is perfectly symmetrical, researchers have long studied the relationship between facial balance and perceived attractiveness, health, and genetic diversity. Our tool uses advanced computer vision to quantify these subtle differences, providing you with a detailed map of your facial structure.
guide.curiosityTitle: 1. Why People are Curious About Symmetry
guide.curiosityDesc: Humans are naturally drawn to symmetry. In nature, symmetry often signals health and genetic fitness. In art and architecture, it creates a sense of harmony and balance. When it comes to the human face, symmetry is often associated with perceived beauty and attractiveness. However, it's important to remember that asymmetry is what makes each face unique and full of character.
guide.curiosityNote: Many people use symmetry analysis to understand their features better, whether for grooming, makeup application, or simply out of curiosity about their own facial geometry.
guide.mythsDesc: There are many misconceptions about facial symmetry. Let's clear up some of the most common ones:
guide.myth1Desc: Reality: Almost everyone has some degree of asymmetry. It's a natural part of human biology.
guide.myth2Desc: Reality: While extreme asymmetry can sometimes be a sign of health issues, minor asymmetry is perfectly normal.
guide.myth3: Myth: Symmetry is the Only Key to Beauty
guide.myth3Desc: Reality: Beauty is subjective and multifaceted. Many famous and attractive people have noticeable asymmetry.
guide.myth4Desc: Reality: Factors like posture, dental work, and even sleeping habits can influence facial balance over time.
guide.tipsDesc: To get the most accurate and consistent results from our AI analysis, follow these practical tips:
guide.tip1Desc: Keep your head level and look directly at the camera. Avoid tilting or turning your head.
guide.tip2Desc: Ensure your face is evenly lit from the front. Avoid harsh side lighting that creates shadows.
guide.tip3Desc: Try to keep your face relaxed and neutral. Smiling or squinting can introduce artificial asymmetry.
guide.tip4Desc: For the best results, try to scan yourself in the same environment and lighting each time.
guide.characterDesc: While it's fun to explore your facial symmetry, remember that your unique features are what make you who you are. Asymmetry is often what gives a face its character, charm, and personality. Use this tool as a way to learn more about your facial structure, but don't let it define your sense of self-worth or beauty.
guide.footerNote: Face Symmetry is an educational and entertainment tool designed to help you understand your facial structure. The results provided are based on mathematical analysis of 2D images and should not be used for medical or diagnostic purposes.

summary.high: Excellent work! Your facial symmetry is highly balanced across all measured areas. Maintain your current healthy habits.

eyes.excellent: Your eye height and spacing are very stable, maintaining good upper face balance.
eyes.good: Your eye positions are generally balanced, and the surrounding structures appear stable.
eyes.slight: There is a slight difference in eye height, and the upper face balance may not be perfectly identical.
eyes.noticeable: There is a noticeable difference in eye height or tilt, and the upper face balance may appear different.

brows.excellent: Your brow height and curves are stably aligned, maintaining very natural upper face balance.
brows.good: Your brow heights are generally balanced, and expression muscle usage appears stable.
brows.slight: There is a slight difference in brow height, which may make the upper face balance look different during expressions.
brows.noticeable: There is a noticeable difference in brow height or angle, and the upper face balance may appear different.

mouth.excellent: Your mouth corner height and surrounding muscle balance are stable, with natural lower face harmony.
mouth.good: Your mouth corner heights are generally balanced, and expression muscle movement appears stable.
mouth.slight: There is a slight difference in mouth corner height, and expression muscle usage patterns may differ.
mouth.noticeable: There is a noticeable difference in mouth corner position or line tilt, making the lower face balance appear different.

jaw.excellent: Your jawline volume and contours are stably maintained, showing very natural lower face balance.
jaw.good: Your jawline balance appears relatively stable, and the lower face structure is naturally aligned.
jaw.slight: There is a slight difference in jawline volume or angle, which may make the lower face balance look different.
jaw.noticeable: There is a noticeable difference in jawline contours, which may emphasize lower face asymmetry.

eyes.low: Significant asymmetry in the eye area often relates to sleeping positions or unconscious squinting. Focus on relaxing the eye muscles and ensure even lighting when working.
eyes.mid: Slight eye level differences are common. Gentle massage around the orbital bone can help release tension and improve symmetry.
eyes.high: Your eye area shows excellent symmetry. Keep maintaining good posture and avoid straining your eyes for long periods.

brows.low: Brow asymmetry is frequently caused by dominant expression habits. Practice neutral expressions and massage the forehead to balance muscle tone.
brows.mid: Minor brow height differences are normal. Consistent facial relaxation exercises can help stabilize the brow line.
brows.high: Your brows are very well-aligned. This contributes significantly to a balanced upper face impression.

mouth.low: Mouth asymmetry often stems from chewing on one side or unilateral expression habits. Try to chew evenly and practice symmetrical smile exercises.
mouth.mid: Small differences in mouth corners are very common. Conscious effort to use both sides of the mouth equally can improve balance.
mouth.high: Your mouth area is highly symmetrical. This indicates balanced muscle usage in the lower face.

jawline.low: Jawline asymmetry can be influenced by dental alignment or sleeping on one side. Consult a professional if you experience tension, and practice jaw relaxation.
jawline.mid: Slight jawline deviations are natural. Avoid resting your chin on your hand and maintain upright posture to support lower face balance.
jawline.high: Your jawline shows great symmetry and definition. This provides a strong, balanced foundation for your facial structure.

overall.excellent: Your overall facial balance is very stable and shows a harmonious structure.
overall.good: Your overall facial balance is within a stable range.
overall.fair: Your overall facial balance is relatively stable, but differences may be observed in some areas.
overall.noticeable: Differences in left-right balance may appear noticeably in some facial areas.
overall.multiple: Differences in left-right balance are observed in several areas.

dynamic.influence: These differences can be influenced by expression habits, muscle usage patterns, or postural factors.

insight.stable:

All measured areas maintain a harmonious balance and are in excellent condition.
Maintain your current lifestyle habits and check your status regularly.

care.title: #### 🎯 Recommended Focus

tips.eyes:
**Orbicularis Oculi** surrounds the eyes and is responsible for expressions.
Massaging gently with fingertips around the corners of the eyes can be helpful.
tips.brows:
**Frontalis** covers the forehead and adjusts brow height.
It's good to massage gently pushing from the center of the forehead towards the temples.
tips.mouth:
**Levator Anguli Oris** is responsible for lifting the corners of the mouth.
Practicing smiles that lift both corners symmetrically while looking in the mirror helps.
tips.jaw:
**Masseter** leads jaw movement and forms the lower contour.
Relaxing the jaw muscle area below the ears by pressing gently helps with symmetry.

general:
**SCM (Sternocleidomastoid)** is responsible for neck alignment and involved in facial symmetry.
Stretching the neck slowly left and right to release tension helps with balance.

**Healthy Habits**: Avoid chewing on only one side or resting your chin on your hand.
Maintaining good posture helps maintain long-term facial symmetry.
care.maintain: We recommend overall facial stretching to maintain your current excellent balance.
care.professional: Facial balance can change naturally depending on lifestyle habits and muscle usage patterns. This report is provided for aesthetic reference, and if you want more systematic balance management, it may be helpful to consult a facial exercise expert or related therapist.

angel.1:
Natural subtle differences are observed between the left and right sides of the face.
These differences are features that appear in most people and can add depth to facial impressions.
This analysis is for reference, and natural balance can make facial impressions look more harmonious.
angel.2:
As a result of facial analysis, natural subtle deviations are observed in left-right balance.
These differences form each individual's unique impression and achieve natural harmony.
Harmonious balance rather than artificial symmetry makes your unique charm stand out more.
angel.3:
The measured asymmetry values are within the daily range and maintain a harmonious impression.
Subtle differences are analyzed as natural elements that add vitality to expressions.
Please use this result only as an aesthetic reference.
angel.4:
Overall facial alignment is analyzed to be maintaining a stable state.
Observed subtle left-right differences appear during the natural development of facial muscles.
Balanced impression is one of the key elements that complete the harmony of the entire face.

potential.high: You are already maintaining a very high level of symmetry. You can maintain near-perfect balance with subtle muscle tension relief.
potential.mid: Maintaining consistent care habits may lead to a slight improvement in your facial balance score.
potential.low: If you correct muscle usage imbalance and combine it with intensive care, there is a high possibility that symmetry will improve significantly.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.zeroStorageDesc: Face Symmetry is designed with a "Privacy by Design" philosophy. Unlike traditional facial analysis tools that upload your photos to a remote server for processing, our application performs all calculations locally on your device. When you grant camera access or upload a photo, the image data stays within your browser's memory. We do not have a backend database that stores your images, and we never transmit your biometric data over the internet.
privacy.noCollectDesc: We believe that your face is your most private data. Therefore, we have built this tool to function without collecting any of the following:
privacy.localProcessingDesc: When you use our AI analysis, your browser uses a technology called WebGL and TensorFlow.js to run the machine learning models directly on your graphics card or processor. This means the "intelligence" happens on your phone or computer, not on our servers. Once you close the tab or refresh the page, all temporary image data is wiped from your device's RAM. There is no persistent storage of your face mesh or analysis results unless you explicitly choose to "Save" or "Download" the result card to your own device.
privacy.analyticsDesc: To improve the user experience and monitor the technical health of the application, we may use basic, anonymized analytics (such as Google Analytics). These tools collect non-identifiable information like browser type, device category, and which features are most used. We do not use tracking cookies for advertising purposes, and we do not sell any data to third parties. Our goal is to build a great tool, not a data profile.
privacy.thirdPartyDesc: Our application uses MediaPipe and TensorFlow.js libraries to perform the facial landmark detection. These libraries are loaded from content delivery networks (CDNs). While these services may log basic request data (like your IP address) to serve the files, they do not receive your camera feed or any image data from our application.
privacy.userControlDesc: By using Face Symmetry, you consent to the local processing of your images for the purpose of symmetry analysis. You can revoke camera permissions at any time through your browser settings. Since we do not store any data, there is no "account" to delete or "data" to request—your privacy is naturally protected by the architecture of the app.
privacy.footerNote: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please feel free to contact us through our official channels. We are committed to transparency and user security.
